title: India · Mid-Cap · Boring Cash Machines · May 2026
analystVoice: Senior value analyst, Khandelwal lens — 'Coal India minus the PSU governance problem'
universe: NSE 500 (504 names pulled from niftyindices.com) ∩ mid-cap band ₹15,000–₹75,000 Cr (254 names) ∩ quant gates (16 names pass-all)
theme: Boring industries (LNG pipelines, mutual fund infra, asset management, diagnostics, pharma exports, specialty chem, mature IT services) where the product is invisible to retail, distribution / regulatory licence is the moat, and cash conversion is the by-product. Coal India's failure mode is the PSU overhang on capital allocation — this screen explicitly filters for **non-PSU promoter / MNC alignment** to avoid that trap.
methodologyNote: Five-stage funnel — fully reproducible: (1) UNIVERSE — pulled NIFTY 500 CSV from niftyindices.com on 2026-05-15; (2) QUANT FETCH — Yahoo Finance via yfinance for all 499 reachable names (5 had no listing data on YF); (3) MKT-CAP BAND — kept 254 in ₹15k–75k Cr; (4) QUANT GATES — 16 names passed ALL 7 gates (mkt-cap band, ROE>18%, D/E<60% of equity, FCF>0, op margin>15%, P/E sanity 0-60×, exclude pure banks); (5) QUALITATIVE OVERLAY — boring theme + non-PSU governance + industry diversification → final 10. Audit trail in scripts/screen_nse500.py + scripts/filter_midcap.py + data/screens/raw/midcap-funnel-2026-05.json.

quality: Mkt cap ₹15,000–75,000 Cr (mid-cap band — wider than typical because India mid-cap inflated post-2024 rally)
Operating Margin > 15% (yfinance operatingMargins — proxy for pricing power)
Trailing P/E in (0, 60) — sanity bound, excludes loss-makers and silly multiples
Exclude pure banks (different metric base — banks measured on NIM, CASA, slippages, not ROCE/op margin)
valuation: P/E reasonable for sector — preferred < 35× general, < 15× for cyclicals (sanity-checked per pick)
Dividend yield > 1% preferred (proves cash returns; not enforced as hard gate so we don't exclude reinvesters)
P/B compared to ROE — high P/B acceptable if ROE > 25% (asset-light franchises)
qualitative: Industry is BORING: not on prime-time TV (LNG terminals, RTA, AMC back-office, diagnostics, specialty chem, mature IT)
Distribution / switching cost / regulatory licence is the moat — not brand-buzz
Governance: family-promoter OR MNC parent OR PSU that passes the explicit PSU framework below (NOT a blanket no-PSU rule — see psuFramework)
Industry diversification: max 2 names per sector to avoid concentration

psuFramework.rationale: PSUs are NOT auto-rejected. Some (Coal India — already in our BUY list — NMDC at the right price, well-run defense PSUs) deserve consideration. The framework below decides per-PSU.
investableWhen: Cost-curve advantage that survives cycles (Coal India coal monopoly, NALCO bauxite, NMDC iron-ore)
Disciplined dividend payout >30% sustained for 5+ years
Govt holding 51-70% (above 75% creates constant OFS overhang on free float)
No history of value-destructive govt-mandated transactions (forced cross-subsidy, capital infusion at lows, PSU-to-PSU bailouts)
Stable management — CMD continuity > 3 years OR organisational depth (BEL-style culture)
Sector is not in structural decline (avoid BSNL/MTNL/Air-India failure modes)
Margin of safety on **normalised earnings** — not peak-cycle EPS at low headline P/E
rejectWhen: Earnings near multi-year peak with cyclical sector (commodity at top of cycle)
Capped regulated returns (NTPC, POWERGRID — fine for income, not for compounding)
Frequent CMD vacancies / turnover (BHEL-style decision paralysis)

appliedExamples.verdict: INVESTABLE — already published as BUY @ ₹462. Coal monopoly, ~50% payout, 51% govt holding, normalised earnings (not peak-cycle), Energy-transition risk priced in.

WATCHLIST — passes most criteria (Panchpatmali bauxite cost-curve advantage, low debt 0.28, ROE 29%, 51% govt) BUT aluminum at cycle peak. On normalised LME aluminum (~$1,800/ton vs current ~$2,500), EPS would be ~40% lower → P/E becomes ~20× → not screamingly cheap. Add at ~25% lower price OR after aluminum cycle correction.
AVOID at price — well-managed defense PSU but P/E 52× pricing in a decade of flawless growth. Late re-rate.
AVOID — ROE 6%, weak balance sheet, P/E 87× on depressed earnings. Classic value-trap.

watchlist.name: National Aluminium Company Ltd. (NALCO)
watchlist.thesis: Best-in-cohort PSU on numbers — vertically integrated (Panchpatmali bauxite, captive power, smelter), low debt, dividend payer.
watchlist.whyWatchlist: Aluminum at cycle peak (LME ~$2,500/ton). Op margin 49.5% is partially cycle-juiced. On normalised earnings, P/E becomes 18-22× — fair, not value.
watchlist.entryTrigger: Buy below ₹150 (~25-30% lower) OR after LME aluminum correction to <$2,000/ton OR if op margin compresses to 30-35% range (mid-cycle).

qualitativeRejects.reason: Moved to WATCHLIST (see psuFramework + watchlist sections). Passes PSU investability framework on most criteria but cyclical-commodity-at-peak is the binding constraint, not PSU governance per se. Buy on aluminum cycle correction.
Quant strong but IT-services AI cannibalisation overhang (see glossary). Multiple is rich at 39× and we already have MPHASIS + COFORGE as IT exposure.
Design-services for autos — quant passes but the auto-OEM ER&D capex cycle showing signs of slowing. 40× P/E vs decelerating growth — not asymmetric.
Auto-software (ADAS / SDV) — hot story, 30× P/E. Same AI/EV cycle bet as TATAELXSI; better to wait for re-rating.
Recently IPO'd (mid-2024) — too short a public track record to underwrite for value.
L&T Tech Services — IT ER&D, similar concerns to PERSISTENT / TATAELXSI. Including would over-concentrate IT exposure.

exclusionsExplained.meaning: Earnings are at a multi-year high (e.g., defense PSU orders growing 40%+ for 5 years on indigenisation push). At peak both **earnings (numerator) AND P/E multiple** are elevated. When the cycle turns, you eat both a margin haircut AND a multiple compression — a double-hit. Rule: buy mid-cycle or in the trough, not at peak.

The market has already re-priced the stock from cheap (12× P/E) to expensive (35× P/E) on good news (e.g., PSU bank re-rating 2022–24). Further upside now requires either (a) earnings over-delivery vs raised expectations or (b) yet another leg of multiple expansion. Easy money is gone; risk-reward asymmetric the wrong way.

For Indian IT-services, the worry that genAI agents will displace billable hours — especially in time-and-materials BPO and maintenance. Even if disruption is slower than feared, the **overhang keeps multiples compressed** until clarity. Better to wait for (a) revenue impact to materialise and stocks to wash out, or (b) AI-immune pockets (ER&D, captive ops) to be priced in distinctly.

Industry-wide pricing power weakens — from new capacity, customer consolidation, or low-cost entrants. EBITDA margins fall; **revenue growth no longer translates to profit growth**. Seen in solar EPC (Adani-led capacity addition), organised retail (aggregator margin take), specialty chems (China capacity returning post-COVID).

Lending businesses earn high ROE in benign credit conditions (low NPAs) and **lose money fast in stressed conditions**. India 2026 shows early NPA stress in unsecured personal loans, MFI, and credit cards. When the cycle visibly turns, ROEs collapse and stocks drop 30–50% (cf. 2018 IL&FS NBFC crisis). Wait for the wash-out before re-entry.

For consumer-facing businesses (QSR, retail, D2C), unit economics = profit per store/order/customer. When these shrink — aggregator commissions rising, dark-kitchen entry, customer-acquisition costs ballooning — the **entire growth narrative falls apart**. You don't even get the consolation of cheap multiple expansion because margin AND growth compress together.

Commodities (steel, chems, paper, cement) move in cycles tied to capacity additions vs demand. Buying when capacity utilisation is at peak + product prices at peak + EBITDA margins at peak = setting up for **50% drawdown** in the next downcycle. The classic 'good business at the wrong time' trap. Wait for capex digestion + price normalisation.

Public-sector undertakings (Coal India, NTPC, NALCO, ONGC) often look statistically cheap with high ROE + low P/E + fat dividend. Government-as-majority-owner introduces real frictions: forced cross-holdings, OFS at unfavourable timing, social-objective pricing (Coal India fuel-supply agreements), and CMD turnover on bureaucratic schedules. Result: PSUs trade at a structural P/E discount to private peers. **But this does NOT mean auto-reject** — see psuFramework above. Coal India clears the framework and sits in our BUY list. The right test is per-PSU on cost-curve advantage, dividend discipline, govt-holding %, and normalised earnings — not a blanket rule.

picks.name: Petronet LNG Ltd.
picks.thesis: Owns India's two largest LNG regasification terminals (Dahej + Kochi) — together ~75% of national LNG regas capacity. JV between GAIL, ONGC, IOC, BPCL (private-domain promoter — not PSU majority).
picks.valueAngle: P/E 10.1× · ROE 18.6% · Div yield 3.7% · D/E 0.11. Cheapest in the pass-list. Tolling business model (take-or-pay) makes earnings recurring; LNG tailwind on India's gas-share push.

picks.name: Computer Age Management Services
picks.thesis: Duopoly with KFin in mutual-fund registrar-and-transfer-agent (RTA) services — handles ~70% of India MF AUM record-keeping. Asset-light, recurring, fee-per-folio model that scales with AUM growth.
picks.valueAngle: P/E 41× is NOT cheap — but ROE 38.7% + 40% op margins + asset-light = re-rating justified by quality. P/B 16× reflects no tangible-asset base. Worth buying on a 20%+ pull-back for long-term compounding.
picks.boringScore: 10/10 — MF folio administration is the dictionary definition of boring

picks.name: Abbott India Ltd.
picks.thesis: Abbott Laboratories (USA) holds 75% — top-tier MNC governance. Strong branded portfolio (Digene, Cremaffin, Thyronorm). Domestic-focused, INR-revenue, FX-shock-immune.
picks.valueAngle: P/E 38× is at the higher end of 10-y range but ROE 34.5% + net cash + zero leverage. Branded generics moat is real (doctor-prescription stickiness). Premium deserved but watch entry price.

picks.name: Nippon Life India AMC
picks.thesis: 5th-largest AMC in India by AUM (~₹6L Cr). Nippon Life Japan owns 73% — clean MNC governance. SIP-led inflow franchise gives AUM stickiness during corrections.
picks.valueAngle: P/E 46.7× is rich but ROE 34.5% + op margin 65% (yes, sixty-five) reflect the operating-leverage of incremental AUM with near-zero variable cost. AMC business will earn 25%+ ROE through cycles. Buy on 25%+ correction.

picks.name: Aditya Birla Sun Life AMC
picks.thesis: JV between Aditya Birla Group and Sun Life Financial (Canada). 7th-largest AMC. Family-conglomerate + MNC JV governance; cleaner than typical Indian promoter family.
picks.valueAngle: P/E 30.6× — meaningfully cheaper than NAM-INDIA + HDFC AMC. ROE 25%, op margin 52%, div yield 2.3% — best value-for-quality in the AMC tier. Smaller AUM = more growth runway.

picks.name: Ajanta Pharma Ltd.
picks.thesis: Family-promoted pharma (Agrawal family, ~70% holding) focused on emerging-markets branded generics + India dermatology/cardiology. Long management tenure. Boring execution-led story.
picks.valueAngle: P/E 37.6× is full but ROE 25% + 23% op margins. 70% promoter holding aligns interests. India + EM-export mix de-risks geography.

picks.name: Dr Lal PathLabs Ltd.
picks.thesis: India's #1 diagnostic chain by quality reputation. Dr Lal family + WestBridge (TPG) backed — clean governance. Asset-light hub-and-spoke model.
picks.valueAngle: P/E 50× looks rich — but the stock has already corrected 40%+ from 2022 highs as online aggregators (Tata 1mg, Pharmeasy) compressed margins. Now stabilising. Industry consolidation tail-wind.

picks.name: Navin Fluorine International
picks.thesis: Niche fluorine chemistry (refrigerants, AHF, specialty molecules for agrochem + pharma intermediates). Promoter (Padma Vibhushan B.K. Birla group → Mafatlal) tenure 60+ years.
picks.valueAngle: P/E 53.7× rich — but stock has corrected ~50% from 2022 highs on chem-cycle reset. ROE 20% + 30% op margin. Wait for cycle-turn confirmation before adding aggressively (see 'Cyclical commodity at peak' glossary entry).

picks.name: Mphasis Ltd.
picks.thesis: BFSI-focused IT services (Citi, JP Morgan, etc.). Blackstone owns 56% — top-tier PE governance. Has been hit by AI cannibalisation fear; price reflects most pessimism.
picks.valueAngle: Cheapest of the IT pass-list: P/E 22× · Div yield 2.9% · ROE 18% · No premium for hyper-growth. If AI disruption is slower than feared, re-rating possible from cheaper base.

picks.name: Coforge Ltd.
picks.thesis: Mid-tier IT specialised in travel-transport-hospitality (SITA, Skywise) + insurance. Baring PE-backed (~40%) → professional management. Niche verticals = stickier client relationships.
picks.valueAngle: P/E 29× — middle of IT pass-list. ROE 18.6% · Op margin 16.6%. Cidara acquisition (May 2024) adds US presence. Watch FY27 margin recovery as integration completes.
